items.label: Getting Started
items.content:
## ZYNQ

ZYNQ is a WebRTC + games framework.
It combines peer-to-peer communication with a game library system.

No backend required.

items.label: Script Tag
items.content:
### Script Tag

Loads ZYNQ globally into `window.ZYNQ`.

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.1/index.min.js"></script>
```

After loading:
```js
window.ZYNQ
```

items.label: ES Module
items.content:
### ES Module (Side Effect Import)

This version does NOT export bindings. It only initializes global `window.ZYNQ`.

```js
import "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.1/index.min.js"
```

After import:
```js
window.ZYNQ.Peer
window.ZYNQ.games
```

items.label: Game Object
items.content:
## Game Object Structure

Represents a single game entry.

```ts
Game {
  name: string,
  alias: string,
  url: string,
  thumb: string,
  category?: string,
  devices?: number[],

  isSupported: boolean,
  isFavorite: boolean
}
```

Example:
```js
{
  name: "Alien Hominid",
  alias: "alien-hominid",
  url: "arcade/alien-hominid",
  thumb: "https://cdn/...",
  category: "shooter"
}
```

children.label: .init()
children.content:
### init(options?)
Loads full game database from CDN.

```js
await ZYNQ.games.init()
```

Options:
```ts
{
  mode?: 'all' | 'supported' | 'favs',
  search?: string,
  sort?: string
}
```

Example:
```js
await ZYNQ.games.init({ search: "alien" })
```

children.label: .search()
children.content:
### search(query)
Filters games by name or alias.

```js
ZYNQ.games.search("alien")
```

Callback result:
```js
ZYNQ.games.list // filtered array
```

children.label: .random()
children.content:
### random(limit)
Returns random subset.

```js
ZYNQ.games.random(3)
```

Result:
```js
ZYNQ.games.list // array<Game>
```

children.label: .getRandomOne()
children.content:
### getRandomOne()
Returns single random game.

```js
const game = ZYNQ.games.getRandomOne()
```

Returns:
```ts
Game | null
```

children.label: .getByAlias()
children.content:
### getByAlias(alias)
Find game by unique id.

```js
const game = ZYNQ.games.getByAlias("alien-hominid")
```

Returns:
```ts
Game | null
```

items.label: Constructor
items.content:
## ZYNQ.Peer
WebRTC abstraction layer on top of PeerJS.

```js
const peer = new ZYNQ.Peer({
  video: true,
  audio: true,
  txt: true
})
```

children.label: .connect()
children.content:
### connect(peerId)
Creates a data connection.

```js
peer.connect("abc-123")
```

children.label: .send()
children.content:
### send(peerId, data)
Sends message to peer.

```js
peer.send("abc-123", "hello")
```

If connection is not ready â†’ message is queued.

---

children.label: .call()
children.content:
### call(peerId, options)
Starts media call.

```js
peer.call("abc-123", {
  video: true,
  audio: true
})
```

children.label: ready
children.content:
### ready
Fired when peer receives its ID from server.

```js
peer.on('ready', (id) => {})
```

Callback:
```ts
id: string
```

Example value:
```js
"c12a9f3e-8b1d-4f0a"
```

children.label: open
children.content:
### open
Fired when connection to another peer is established.

```js
peer.on('open', (peerId) => {})
```

Callback:
```ts
peerId: string
```

Example:
```js
"dc560603-9e11-4d8d"
```

children.label: message
children.content:
### message
Triggered when data arrives.

```js
peer.on('message', (msg) => {})
```

Callback object:
```ts
msg = {
  from: string,
  type: "data",
  data: any
}
```

Example:
```js
{
  from: "peer-123",
  type: "data",
  data: "hello"
}
```

children.label: stream
close
children.content:
### close
Connection fully closed.

```js
peer.on('close', ({ id }) => {})
```

Callback:
```ts
{
  id: string
}
```

children.label: error
children.content:
### error
Error handler.

```js
peer.on('error', (err) => {})
```

Error object:
```ts
{
  type: 'peer' | 'data' | 'media' | 'reconnect_failed',
  id?: string,
  error: any
}
```

children.label: reconnecting
children.content:
### reconnecting
Triggered when auto reconnect happens.

```js
peer.on('reconnecting', ({ id, attempt, delay }) => {})
```

Callback:
```ts
{
  id: string,
  attempt: number,
  delay: number
}
```

Example:
```js
{
  id: "peer-123",
  attempt: 2,
  delay: 1200
}
```
